key_highlights: S&P 500 futures fall after mixed Big Tech earnings, with Alphabet and Amazon beating but Meta sliding on AR/VR spending concerns.
Iran war escalation drives oil and gas prices to multi-year highs, with Brent surging and US gas prices hitting $4.39/gallon.
Fed holds rates steady, citing Iran-fueled inflation, while Powell stays on board amid DOJ probe.
Apple delivers a near-perfect quarter with CEO change and AI update ahead, bucking tech weakness.
Intel hits all-time high on AI chip demand, while Samsung chip profit jumps 50-fold on supply shortage outlook.
Euro zone inflation jumps to 3% with near-stalled growth, adding to global stagflation fears.
Japan intervenes in yen with ~$34.5B, but analysts see only short-term fix.
UAE exits OPEC amid Gulf tensions, exposing US shale to new rival.
Cathie Wood buys $18M of beaten-down AI stock, signaling contrarian bet.
Bitcoin moving from trade to treasury asset, with Saylor declaring next big bang close.

sectors.trend: Mixed earnings with divergence: Apple and Intel shine, Meta and Shopify face headwinds, AI spending remains central but OpenAI misses targets cause selloff.
sectors.implications: AI capex cycle intact but scrutiny on ROI increases; Apple's CapEx-light AI strategy gains favor; semiconductor supply shortage to worsen in 2027 benefits Samsung and NXP.

sectors.trend: Oil and gas prices surge to multi-year highs due to Iran blockade and UAE OPEC exit, boosting Exxon and Chevron profits but straining global economy.
sectors.implications: Energy sector outperforms with war-driven rally; Chevron CEO warns of extreme stress; US gas prices hit $4.39/gallon, fueling inflation and consumer strain.

sectors.trend: Fed holds rates steady, Powell stays on board; JPMorgan faces scandal; Blackstone Mortgage Trust reports earnings; bond traders brace for Gilts selloff.
sectors.implications: Higher-for-longer rates pressure real estate and regional banks; JPMorgan lawsuit adds reputational risk; UK local elections could trigger bond volatility.

sectors.trend: Amazon and Uber expand services (hotel bookings), Carvana and Chipotle report earnings; gas price surge threatens consumer spending.
sectors.implications: Travel and e-commerce resilient but rising energy costs may dampen discretionary spending; Chipotle and Carvana show mixed results.

sectors.trend: Teva Pharmaceuticals soars 12% on record profits; Waystar and Glaukos report earnings; Orthocell enters Canadian market.
sectors.implications: Generic pharma and med-tech show strength; healthcare remains defensive amid macro uncertainty.

sectors.trend: National housing correction begins in Australia; Blackstone Mortgage Trust reports; US housing market under pressure from high rates.
sectors.implications: Rising rates and affordability crisis drive price declines; commercial real estate faces refinancing risks.

sectors.trend: Caterpillar reports earnings; aluminum gains on Iran blockade; BWX Technologies highlighted as sole USN nuclear supplier.
sectors.implications: Defense and infrastructure spending supportive; commodity price volatility impacts margins.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin moving from trade to treasury asset; Riot Platforms reports earnings; Saylor declares next big bang close.
sectors.implications: Institutional adoption continues; Bitcoin as reserve asset narrative strengthens; mining stocks volatile.

key_themes: Iran war escalation and energy price shock
Big Tech AI capex divergence and ROI scrutiny
Fed hold and Powell's extended tenure amid DOJ probe
Global stagflation risks (Euro zone inflation 3%, near-stalled growth)
UAE OPEC exit reshapes oil market dynamics
Japan yen intervention and currency volatility
AI disruption and worker support policies (Singapore PM)
Housing correction in Australia and US affordability crisis
Bitcoin as treasury asset gaining traction
Political pressure on media (ABC, FCC license reviews)
China rare earth leverage and factory activity slowdown

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran War Escalation and Energy Supply Disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks (XOM, CVX) and commodities; hedge with gold or inflation-protected bonds; reduce discretionary consumer exposure.
Favor defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities) and real assets; reduce duration in bond portfolios; consider TIPS and commodities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Big Tech AI Capex Overspend and ROI Uncertainty
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify within tech; favor companies with CapEx-light AI strategies (AAPL) over heavy spenders (META); monitor OpenAI targets.
Hedge FX exposure in portfolios; avoid long-dated JPY bonds; monitor BOJ actions and EM capital flows.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Housing Market Correction and CRE Refinancing Risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to regional banks and REITs; focus on high-quality mortgage servicers; avoid overleveraged real estate plays.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical Tensions (UAE OPEC Exit, China Rare Earths, Taiwan)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify supply chains; invest in rare earth alternatives and defense stocks (BWXT); monitor Taiwan Strait developments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and Political Risks (FCC, SCOTUS, China Vehicle Ban)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid media stocks with regulatory overhang; monitor trade policy changes; consider domestic manufacturing beneficiaries.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Apple (AAPL) on weakness as CapEx-light AI winner with near-perfect quarter and CEO transition clarity.
opportunities.rationale: Apple's AI strategy avoids heavy capex, strong services growth, and upcoming AI update positions it as a defensive tech leader.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Intel (INTC) on AI chip demand and all-time high momentum, with semiconductor supply shortage tailwind.
opportunities.rationale: Intel's foundry pivot and AI chip demand driving record highs; supply shortage into 2027 supports pricing power.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy stocks (XOM, CVX) for war-driven oil rally and strong earnings beats.
opportunities.rationale: Iran blockade and UAE OPEC exit support elevated oil prices; Exxon and Chevron beat estimates with strong cash flows.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Teva Pharmaceuticals (TEVA) on record profits and defensive healthcare positioning.
opportunities.rationale: Record profits and 12% surge indicate strong execution; healthcare defensive in stagflation scenario.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Bitcoin (BTC) exposure as treasury asset narrative gains traction and institutional adoption grows.
opportunities.rationale: Bitcoin moving from trade to treasury asset; Saylor's bullish call and Riot earnings support positive outlook.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) on chip profit surge and supply shortage outlook.
opportunities.rationale: 50-fold profit jump and worsening supply shortage into 2027 make Samsung a key beneficiary of AI-driven memory demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary stocks amid gas price surge and housing correction.
defensive_moves.rationale: Gas prices at 4-year high ($4.39/gallon) squeeze consumer budgets; housing correction in Australia signals broader weakness.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against inflation with TIPS, gold, and commodity ETFs.
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran war fuels inflation, Fed holds rates, Euro zone inflation at 3%; real assets protect purchasing power.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios and avoid long-dated Gilts ahead of UK local elections.
defensive_moves.rationale: Bond traders brace for Gilts selloff; local elections could trigger policy uncertainty and higher yields.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Meta (META) until AR/VR spending clarity and AI ROI improves.
defensive_moves.rationale: Meta stock falls despite strong earnings due to continued AR/VR cash burn; OpenAI miss adds to tech sentiment headwinds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify away from overvalued AI stocks with high capex; favor CapEx-light AI beneficiaries.
defensive_moves.rationale: AI spending scrutiny increases after OpenAI misses targets; Apple's model shows lower risk path.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets face headwinds from Iran war escalation, rising energy prices, and stagflation fears. S&P 500 futures signal pause after strong April. Fed hold and Powell's extended tenure provide stability but no easing. Tech sector divergence will persist with AI capex scrutiny. Oil and gas will remain elevated, supporting energy stocks but pressuring consumers. Yen intervention offers temporary relief but structural weakness remains. Housing correction in Australia and US affordability crisis may deepen. Overall, defensive positioning and commodity exposure recommended.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI wave continues to drive semiconductor and tech growth, with supply shortages into 2027 benefiting Samsung and Intel. Energy transition and geopolitical realignment (UAE OPEC exit) reshape oil markets. Bitcoin adoption as treasury asset could accelerate. Stagflation risks may force Fed to eventually cut if growth stalls, but inflation persistence limits room. Emerging markets face currency and capital flow challenges. Long-term winners: AI infrastructure, energy, healthcare, and digital assets.
key_catalysts: Iran war developments and potential ceasefire or escalation
Fed policy path and Powell's board tenure resolution
Big Tech AI capex plans and ROI realization (OpenAI targets)
Euro zone inflation and ECB response
Japan yen intervention effectiveness and BOJ policy shift
UAE OPEC exit impact on oil supply and prices
US housing market data and affordability crisis
China rare earth export controls and factory activity
Bitcoin halving and institutional adoption trends
UK local elections and Gilts market reaction
SCOTUS ruling on Montgomery broker case
AI regulation and worker support policies globally
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and US gas prices daily
Fed speeches and FOMC minutes for rate path clues
Big Tech earnings reactions and AI spending commentary
Euro zone GDP and inflation data
US housing starts and existing home sales
UAE-OPEC relations and Iran blockade updates
Semiconductor equipment orders and lead times
Corporate bond spreads and credit default swaps
Political developments (FCC, ABC license, China vehicle ban)
